_count: Maximum results per upstream page — controls response size and token usage but does not make a paginated result complete
_sort: Sort order for results (e.g. -date for newest first, birthdate for oldest first)
_summary: Return summarized resources: true (metadata only), text (text + id + meta), count (count only), data (remove text)
_elements: Comma-separated list of element names to include in each resource (e.g. name,birthDate)
_include: Include referenced resources in the response Bundle (e.g. Observation:patient)
_revinclude: Include resources that reference the matched resources (e.g. Provenance:target)
_lastUpdated: Filter by when the resource was last changed (e.g. ge2024-01-01) — useful for 'changed since' queries; server-gated per resource
fhirpath: FHIRPath expression evaluated locally against the FHIR response — use for client-side projection when _elements or _summary are unavailable or insufficient
responseMode: Response shape: compact (token-efficient) or full (raw FHIR JSON)
maxResults: Target number of compact results for server-side page coalescing — set high enough for the requested range; a next link after coalescing means more data exists beyond this batch
prefetch: Set to false to disable server-side page coalescing and return a single upstream page; single-page results may be incomplete when a next link is present
